campaigns.company: BNY Mellon Municipal Bond Infrastructure Fund, Inc.
campaigns.campaigner: Saba Capital Master Fund, Ltd. (Saba Capital)
campaigns.ask: Board seat(s) and a proposal to declassify the Fund's Board.
campaigns.summary: Saba Capital has nominated an individual for election to the Fund's Board and is backing a charter amendment to declassify the board, stemming from a prior non-binding activist proposal. The Fund's Board unanimously opposes both the declassification proposal and Saba's director nominee, urging stockholders to vote against them.

campaigns.campaigner: Al Shams Investments LTD (Wafic Rida Said)
campaigns.ask: Board seats via proxy contest at the annual meeting
campaigns.summary: Al Shams Investments LTD, together with Wafic Rida Said, is soliciting proxies from Braemar Hotels & Resorts shareholders using a WHITE universal proxy card in connection with the company's annual meeting, indicating an active proxy fight for board representation.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and affiliated Partici
campaigns.ask: Solicit shareholder agent designations to call a special meeting of shareholders.
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, a large beneficial owner (~146.5M shares via Vicksburg Investments and the Melissa A. Neugebauer 2020 Trust), together with a slate of proposed participants including David Daglio, Charles Elson, and others, has filed definitive proxy materials soliciting shareholder support to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer (Vicksburg Investments Management LLC, Melissa A. Neugebauer
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders to vote on board composition and strategic direction, citing the company's failure to hold an annual shareholder meeting.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., is soliciting shareholder consents (via GREEN cards) to force a special meeting, backed by recommendations from Glass Lewis and Egan-Jones. He is hosting a live analyst town hall, accusing Fermi leadership (including Gov. Rick Perry and COO Jacobo Ortiz) of avoiding shareholder accountability and suppressing shareholder rights, and rece

campaigns.company: XAI Floating Rate & Alternative Income Trust
campaigns.campaigner: Octagon Credit Investors, LLC
campaigns.ask: Vote AGAINST the proposed replacement of Octagon with Rockford Tower Asset Management (King Street Capital) as the Fund's investment sub-adviser.
campaigns.summary: Octagon, the Fund's sub-adviser since its 2017 IPO, is urging shareholders to reject the Board's decision to replace it with Rockford Tower (a King Street Capital subsidiary), arguing the change is ill-advised and that shareholders are not receiving clear benefits while accepting transition risk.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders to unlock value (likely seeking board/strategic change)
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, a large shareholder controlling ~146.5 million shares, is leading a group of participants to solicit shareholder support to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. The group has filed a definitive proxy statement and is actively campaigning via a dedicated website and social media to unlock shareholder value.

campaigns.company: XAI Floating Rate & Alternative Income Trust
campaigns.campaigner: Octagon Credit Investors, LLC
campaigns.ask: Vote AGAINST the proposed new sub-advisory agreement that would replace Octagon with Rockford Tower Asset Management as the Fund's investment sub-adviser.
campaigns.summary: Octagon, XFLT's sub-adviser since 2017, is waging a proxy fight urging shareholders to reject the Board's decision to replace it with Rockford Tower, a newly formed King Street subsidiary with no closed-end fund track record. Octagon argues the switch is not in shareholders' best interests and that the Board made a 'monumental error of judgment.'

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer (Vicksburg Investments Management LLC)
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting to expand the board and elect seven independent directors, and reverse a 70% supermajority bylaw amendment.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest shareholder, is waging a proxy fight to replace the board after it cancelled a special meeting and imposed a 70% supermajority voting requirement to block board expansion. A Texas Business Court has granted his motion for expedited discovery to investigate the board's entrenchment tactics.

campaigns.campaigner: Bradley L. Radoff and Jumana Capital Investments LLC (the Radoff-Jumana Group)
campaigns.ask: Replace two long-tenured directors with the activist's two nominees and implement governance reforms including separating the Chair/CEO roles, increasing share repurchases, exploring sale-leasebacks, and evaluating dives
campaigns.summary: The Radoff-Jumana Group, holding ~8.7% of GCO, is waging a proxy fight to remove two legacy directors (Thurgood Marshall Jr. and Joanna Barsh) who have overseen roughly -50% TSR during their 13-14 year tenures, arguing the board lacks accountability under CEO/Chair/Interim CFO Mimi Vaughn. The group is nominating Westervelt T. Ballard Jr. and Paula J. Poskon to bring operational, capital allocatio

campaigns.campaigner: Bradley L. Radoff and Michael Torok (Radoff-JEC Group)
campaigns.ask: Elect three director nominees (Berman, Horowitz, Rinaldini) to the Board to oversee a strategic review and prevent further value destruction.
campaigns.summary: The Radoff-JEC Group, owning approximately 7.7% of Seer, is waging a proxy fight to replace the Chair/CEO and two other directors, citing over $310 million in cash burn, a 97% share price decline, excessive executive compensation, and the adoption of an anti-stockholder poison pill.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC (and affiliated Partic
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders via agent designations (proxy fight).
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, a large beneficial owner (~146.5M shares) and founder-affiliated party, is soliciting shareholder agent designations to force a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The campaign group includes several named participants (Daglio, Elson, Jimenez, Yang, Hooda, Pujadas) and is distributing proxy materials via social media.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Board change / strategic alternatives process — calling for a special meeting of shareholders, an independent banker-led dual-track strategic alternatives process (sale vs. current path) for Project Matador, and challeng
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest individual shareholder, is waging a proxy fight to call a special shareholder meeting, proposing a town hall debate with co-founders Gov. Rick Perry and Griffin Perry. He alleges the board entrenched itself with a 70% supermajority threshold after losing in federal court, and demands an independent, banker-led strategic alternatives process to evaluate a

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Call a Special Meeting of Shareholders to elect a 100% independent board slate and restore governance, with a mandate to evaluate all strategic alternatives including a full-value change-of-control process.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest individual shareholder, is soliciting shareholders to call a special meeting to replace the current board, which he accuses of unprecedented bad governance (supermajority requirements, undisclosed committees, mid-contest rule changes). He argues that even if a tenant deal is imminent, shareholders deserve a board that will honestly evaluate all strategic

campaigns.campaigner: YZILabs Management Ltd.
campaigns.ask: Board seats, CEO replacement, and strategic governance changes via consent solicitation (subsequently settled)
campaigns.summary: YZILabs Management launched a consent solicitation to expand CEA Industries' board and elect new directors, but reached a cooperation agreement on June 23, 2026, under which the company appointed three YZi Labs nominees to the board, formed a CEO search committee, and installed a YZi Labs nominee as Interim President. The activist withdrew its consent solicitation as part of the settlement.

campaigns.campaigner: Bradley L. Radoff / Radoff-JEC Group
campaigns.ask: Board seats for three independent director nominees and adoption of stockholder-friendly governance/sale process
campaigns.summary: The Radoff-JEC Group, holding ~7.7% of Seer, is waging a proxy contest to replace three directors, citing a -97% post-IPO share price decline, $465M+ in cumulative losses, and CEO enrichment amid value destruction. The group also made multiple acquisition offers at 33-42% premiums and a June 15 settlement proposal (tender offer at $2.50/share plus governance reforms), which the Board has ignored.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Call a special shareholder meeting; soliciting proxy designations (GREEN card) to challenge the board.
campaigns.summary: Founder Toby Neugebauer, controlling ~146.5M shares, and a slate of participants including David Daglio, Charles Elson, and others are waging a proxy fight to force a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, signaling an effort to reshape board control or corporate direction.

campaigns.campaigner: UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC)
campaigns.ask: Vote against TWO's proposed merger with CrossCountry Mortgage (CCM) and instead engage with UWMC's superior acquisition offer, which includes an option to elect $12.50 per share in cash or 2.3328 shares of UWMC stock per
campaigns.summary: UWMC is waging a proxy campaign urging TWO stockholders to vote against the CCM merger at the June 23 special meeting, arguing its offer provides higher value and stockholder choice. All three major proxy advisors (ISS, Glass Lewis, Egan-Jones) have recommended against the CCM transaction, and UWMC is pressing the TWO Board to engage in good-faith negotiations.

campaigns.campaigner: UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC)
campaigns.ask: Vote AGAINST the proposed merger with CrossCountry (CCM) and engage with UWMC on its superior competing acquisition offer.
campaigns.summary: UWMC is soliciting TWO shareholders to vote against the proposed CCM merger, arguing its own $12.50/share cash or 2.3328 UWMC shares offer is superior. TWO's special meeting has already been adjourned twice and postponed once due to insufficient shareholder support for the CCM deal.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer (Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and affiliates)
campaigns.ask: Board seats via independent nominees and an immediate dual-track strategic review to maximize shareholder value, including a strategic transaction and replacement of senior leadership.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest shareholder, is waging a proxy fight against the incumbent board, nominating independent directors and pushing for a dual-track strategic review. He alleges the board has spread falsehoods and mismanaged the company since his termination, and calls on shareholders—not PR operatives—to decide the company's future.

campaigns.campaigner: Daniel P. Houle and the Concerned Vaxart Stockholders
campaigns.ask: Board seats — seeking election of a dissident slate of director nominees at the 2026 annual meeting.
campaigns.summary: Daniel P. Houle and a group of Concerned Vaxart Stockholders have launched a proxy fight, filing a definitive proxy statement and GOLD universal proxy card to elect their slate of director nominees at Vaxart's 2026 annual meeting, accompanied by an investor presentation titled 'A Case for Change at Vaxart.'

campaigns.campaigner: Murchinson Ltd.
campaigns.ask: Board declassification, shareholder-rights-plan restriction, major-transaction approval requirement, and removal/replacement of certain directors at an extraordinary general meeting.
campaigns.summary: Murchinson (7.7% holder) is soliciting proxies for an extraordinary general meeting to overhaul Nano Dimension's governance and board, citing underperformance, poor governance, and value-destructive decisions.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC (Fermi Founder Parties)
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders to vote on proposals regarding the future of Fermi Inc.
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, founder and large shareholder (~146.5M shares), is soliciting shareholder consent to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, framing it as a 'good governance vote' on the company's future. The filing is a definitive additional proxy material urging street-name shareholders to vote their GREEN agent designation cards to consent to the special meeting.

campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders (soliciting agent designations to force a special meeting).

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC (Fermi Founder Parties
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders, seeking board representation and strategic change
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer and affiliated participants are soliciting shareholder proxies (green cards) to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. Neugebauer beneficially owns ~146.5 million shares and is leading a proxy fight alongside a slate of proposed directors.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and affiliated Partici
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders via solicitation of agent designations (proxy fight).
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, a large shareholder beneficially owning ~146.5 million shares, together with a slate of proposed participants including David Daglio, Charles Elson, and others, is soliciting shareholder support to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The group has filed a definitive proxy statement and green agent designation cards with the SEC.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer (Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and affiliated particip
campaigns.ask: Replace the current board and management; install John Sellers of Double Eagle as Chairman and CEO and pursue strategic transactions with hyperscalers/technology companies.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer is waging a proxy fight to replace Fermi's board and management, alleging the current board is breaching fiduciary duty by refusing to engage with potential strategic acquirers and partners despite the stock being 'woefully undervalued.' He proposes John Sellers of Double Eagle Energy as CEO/Chairman as a compromise to restore credibility with capital markets and customers, while contin

campaigns.campaigner: Diana Shipping Inc.
campaigns.ask: Acquire all outstanding GNK shares for $27.34 per share ($24.80 cash + 1 Diana share), and delay the annual meeting to allow engagement.
campaigns.summary: Diana Shipping, in partnership with Star Bulk Carriers, raised its non-binding offer to acquire Genco Shipping & Trading at a 53% premium to the undisturbed price and a 6% premium to NAV, with $1.433B in committed financing. Diana is pressing the Genco board to delay the June 18 annual meeting to evaluate the revised proposal.

campaigns.campaigner: Diana Shipping Inc. (DSX)
campaigns.ask: Elect two independent director nominees (Jens Ismar and Paul Cornell), withhold on two incumbent directors, and vote against ratification of Genco's poison pill and equity incentive plan; also pushing for access to a $24
campaigns.summary: Diana Shipping, Genco's largest shareholder (~14.93%), is waging a proxy contest ahead of Genco's June 18, 2026 annual meeting, arguing the Genco board has entrenched management and blocked a fully financed $24.80/share cash offer through a poison pill and a change-in-control retention plan adopted without shareholder approval.

campaigns.campaigner: UWM Holdings Corporation (UWMC)
campaigns.ask: Accept UWMC's superior acquisition proposal over the pending CrossCountry Mortgage merger and engage in good-faith negotiations.
campaigns.summary: UWMC is waging a proxy/M&A contest to replace TWO's pending CCM merger with its own cash-and-stock offer (at $12.50/share cash election plus stock optionality), accusing the TWO board of erecting arbitrary restrictions to avoid genuine engagement and ignoring stockholder opposition to the CCM deal after three adjourned special meetings.

campaigns.campaigner: Al Shams Investments LTD (ASIL) / Wafic Rida Said
campaigns.ask: Board seats / proxy contest at the upcoming Annual Meeting
campaigns.summary: ASIL, together with Wafic Rida Said, issued a press release and letter to Braemar's outside directors and filed a Schedule 13D amendment, indicating plans to run a proxy contest with a WHITE Universal Proxy Card at the company's annual meeting.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders and solicit agent designations (proxy fight for control/governance change)
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer and affiliated participants are soliciting shareholder agent designations to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc., filing a definitive proxy statement and green proxy card. Neugebauer beneficially owns ~146.5 million shares of Fermi common stock.

campaigns.campaigner: Diana Shipping Inc.
campaigns.ask: Board seats for two nominees (Jens Ismar and Paul Cornell), rescission of poison pill, and support for its all-cash premium takeover offer
campaigns.summary: Diana Shipping, Genco's largest shareholder, is waging a proxy fight to replace Genco directors and dismantle a poison pill that ISS has recommended shareholders vote against. Diana has made a fully financed all-cash premium offer for Genco and argues the board is entrenching itself rather than engaging with the offer.

campaigns.campaigner: Bradley L. Radoff and Michael Torok (Radoff-JEC Group)
campaigns.ask: Elect three independent director nominees to replace current board members at the 2026 annual meeting, citing 97% share price destruction since IPO.
campaigns.summary: The Radoff-JEC Group, holding ~7.7% of Seer, is waging a proxy fight to replace three directors, arguing that CEO Omid Farokhzad has a track record of value destruction across multiple companies. The group is urging shareholders to vote against Farokhzad and two other incumbents at the July 28, 2026 annual meeting.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC (and affiliated Partic
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders via proxy solicitation (GREEN agent designations card), seeking to unlock shareholder value at Fermi Inc.
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, a large shareholder beneficially owning ~146.5M shares (~through Vicksburg Investments and the Melissa A. Neugebauer 2020 Trust), along with a slate of Participants including David Daglio, Charles Elson, John Jimenez, Janet Yang, Sheila Hooda, and Juan Pujadas, has filed a definitive proxy statement soliciting agent designations to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC (Fermi Founder Parties
campaigns.ask: Call a special meeting of shareholders via proxy solicitation (GREEN agent designations card), seeking board change/strategic direction.
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer, a founder and large shareholder (~146.5M shares), along with a slate of proposed director candidates (Daglio, Elson, Jimenez, Yang, Hooda, Pujadas), is soliciting shareholder agent designations to force a special meeting at Fermi Inc. The group has filed a definitive proxy statement and is urging shareholders to support the call for a special meeting.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation: systematic 'license renting' scheme at ~20% of skilled nursing facilities, constituting fraud against Medicare/Medicaid and inflating margins/growth.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters alleges Ensign rents the licenses of absent Administrators at roughly 20% of its SNFs, enabling facilities to bill Medicare/Medicaid despite lacking legally required on-site licensed management. It estimates compliance would cut 2027 EBIT ~35% versus consensus and expose the company to multi-billion-dollar False Claims Act sanctions.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer (Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and affiliates)
campaigns.ask: Call a Special Meeting to elect a new independent Board majority committed to a full-value strategic review/sale process.
campaigns.summary: Neugebauer, Fermi's co-founder and largest shareholder, is soliciting shareholders to call a Special Meeting to replace the unelected board majority with six independent directors who would run a rigorous sales process, accusing the current board of avoiding accountability and blanket-rejecting a sale without testing the market.

campaigns.campaigner: Diana Shipping Inc. (DSX)
campaigns.ask: Two board seats (Jens Ismar and Paul Cornell), vote against poison pill ratification and equity incentive plan
campaigns.summary: Diana Shipping, Genco's largest shareholder, is running a proxy fight to elect two independent directors to Genco's board and defeat ratification of Genco's poison pill and equity incentive plan, citing board entrenchment and excessive compensation. ISS has recommended shareholders vote against both governance proposals.

campaigns.campaigner: Al Shams Investments LTD (ASIL) / Wafic Rida Said
campaigns.ask: Board seats via proxy contest at the upcoming annual meeting, accompanied by public pressure on outside directors.
campaigns.summary: ASIL, together with Wafic Rida Said, is waging a proxy fight against Braemar Hotels & Resorts, soliciting shareholders with a WHITE universal proxy card to elect its slate of director nominees at the annual meeting. The campaign follows a public letter to the board's outside directors and an amended 13D filing, signaling dissatisfaction with the company's current governance or strategic direction.

campaigns.campaigner: Toby R. Neugebauer / Vicksburg Investments Management LLC
campaigns.ask: Solicit proxies to call a special meeting of shareholders.
campaigns.summary: Toby Neugebauer and affiliated parties are soliciting shareholder proxies and agent designations to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, anticipated to be held around June 30, 2026.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Alleges SRAD systematically aids and abets illegal gambling operators in banned markets, contradicting management's compliance/KYC claims, with illegal operators estimated at 20-40% of revenues.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short SRAD, citing undercover investigations, source-code analysis, and employee testimony showing the company actively supplies illegal gambling operators across black/grey markets (China, Southeast Asia, Russia) while CEO Carsten Koerl publicly claims rigorous compliance. The report identifies ~50 current/recent illegal-market clients, including organized-crime- and human-traffic

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation of accounting fraud: SoFi booked a $312M borrowing from JPMorgan as a loan sale, inflating profits and management bonuses, with a restatement likely to erase ~$1B of reported EBITDA and materially lower capita
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short SoFi, alleging the company misclassified a $312M JPMorgan borrowing as a loan sale in Q3 2024, which inflated earnings and executive compensation while shareholders face ~15% annual dilution. The firm believes Utah UCC filings contradict SoFi's accounting and expects a restatement that would also reduce ~$1B of previously reported EBITDA and materially lower capital ratios.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation that SoFi's 2025 Adjusted EBITDA is inflated by ~90% and that executives have extracted significant proceeds via prepaid variable forward contracts.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short SoFi, alleging the company's 2025 Adjusted EBITDA is inflated by approximately 90% and criticizing management for extracting $58.3M via prepaid variable forward contracts while failing to substantively address the short report's factual claims.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation of accounting manipulation and financial engineering, including material misstatement of at least $312 million of unrecorded debt, inflated fair value gains, and disguised borrowings as revenue/fee income.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters alleges SoFi is a financial engineering treadmill that dilutes shareholders to fund management bonuses through GE Capital-style loan marks and Enron-esque off-balance-sheet structures. The short firm claims SoFi's true personal loan charge-off rate is ~6.1% vs. reported 2.89%, and that its Loan Platform Business is a disguised form of borrowing booked as fee income rather than a capit

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation that AppLovin CEO and CTO made false statements denying the company's use of persistent identifiers that violate user privacy and partner platform terms of service.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters, short AppLovin, alleges that management's denials regarding the use of persistent identifiers are demonstrably false and misleading, following the firm's initial March 27, 2025 report.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation: AppLovin's e-commerce conversions are largely low-incrementality retargeting, and it is violating platform partners' terms of service by collecting/structuring user IDs, risking deplatforming and competitive
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short AppLovin, alleging ~52% of APP's e-commerce conversions are retargeting with only ~25-35% incrementality, and that APP appears to violate key platform TOS by harvesting user IDs, risking deplatforming and client churn.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation that FTAI violated U.S. OFAC sanctions by supplying aircraft engine parts to an Iranian MRO entity.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters presents photographic and video evidence from LinkedIn posts by an Iranian airline executive showing CFM-56 engines and FTAI packaging at Sorena Turbine in Iran, suggesting FTAI's Aerospace Products business supplied parts in violation of U.S. sanctions. The short firm notes that OFAC violations carry strict liability and potentially severe penalties, including prison time.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation: Misleading financial reporting by classifying one-time whole engine sales as MRO revenue to inflate valuation.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short FTAI Aviation, alleging the company misleads investors by classifying one-time whole engine sales as Maintenance Repair & Overhaul (MRO) revenue to artificially inflate its valuation. The short seller estimates ~80% of the Aerospace Products segment's adjusted EBITDA is gains on these asset sales, which Fortress capitalized on via a May 2024 secondary offering.

campaigns.campaigner: Muddy Waters
campaigns.ask: Allegation that ELF materially overstated revenue by ~$135M–$190M over the past three quarters and inflated inventory, including deceiving its auditor.
campaigns.summary: Muddy Waters is short ELF, alleging the company began inflating revenue and profits in Q2 FY24 as inventory built and its growth narrative faltered. The firm contends ELF's claim that a sourcing-practice change caused a $36.9M inventory jump is false, and that the real cause was weak sales—indicating potential auditor deception.
